title: Actor Identity
description: An identity record in the auth system. The product owns the data format — the auth provider stores data in this format, not its own. This ensures migration between providers, platform-level swaps, export with Your Memory, and owner inspection. The type field is extensible — new actor types are new values, not schema changes. Source: auth-spec.md (D22, D27, D60).

id.description: Unique identifier for this actor. Stable across sessions and exports. Used in X-Actor-ID headers, audit logs, and policy subjects.

type.description: The type of actor. This enum is extensible — new actor types are new values, not schema changes. V1 implements only 'owner' and 'system_agent'. The identity schema supports all types from day one so that adding new actor types is a data change, not a schema change. Types: owner (the person whose system this is, exactly one per instance), collaborator (human invited by owner with scoped access, V2), system_agent (interactive AI agent acting on owner's behalf, V1), background_agent (scheduled tasks like digest and gardener, V1.1), external_agent (another AI connecting from outside, V3), service (integration like calendar sync or webhook, V3), economic (can spend money, V3), federated (another system instance, V4).

display_name.description: Human-readable name for this actor. Shown in conversation history, audit logs, and permission management. E.g., 'Dave W', 'Daily Digest Agent', 'Calendar Sync Service'.

status.description: Current status of this identity. active: can authenticate and act. suspended: temporarily disabled, can be reactivated. revoked: permanently disabled, access revoked. Revoking an active actor terminates any in-progress operations immediately.

created.description: When this identity was created (ISO 8601 timestamp). Included in exports for provenance.
